## Gossip spreads transactions and blocks through repeated local relay

Peer-to-peer structure explains who is connected to whom. Gossip explains how
information moves across those connections.

In a gossip-style protocol, a node that learns about a new transaction or block
forwards it to some or all of its peers. Those peers then repeat the process. The
whole network does not need a central broadcaster. Global dissemination emerges from
many local forwarding decisions.

## Why gossip is useful

The main advantage is robustness through simple repeated relay.

- A node only needs to know a limited set of peers.
- Messages can travel through many alternative paths.
- The system can keep working even if some links fail or some peers disappear.

This is a pragmatic fit for decentralized systems, where the network must keep
operating without assuming any single reliable router.

## Why gossip also creates duplicate traffic

The same message may reach a node multiple times from different peers. That is not a
bug by itself. Redundancy is part of what makes gossip resilient.

Nodes therefore need lightweight bookkeeping, for example remembering message ids they
have already seen, so they relay new information once rather than flooding the
network forever.

## How peer discovery fits in

Gossip only works after a node has peers. Peer discovery is the process of finding an
initial set of network neighbors.

In practice, systems may use:

- bootstrap peers,
- DNS seeds,
- cached addresses from prior sessions,
- or protocol-level address exchange.

Students do not need production detail yet. The core point is that "who do I connect
to first?" is a real systems problem, not a trivial footnote.

## What students should be able to explain

After this concept, students should be able to explain:

- how gossip relays transactions and blocks without a central broadcaster,
- why duplicate message receipt is expected in resilient peer-to-peer systems,
- why nodes track already-seen messages,
- and why peer discovery is necessary before message propagation can begin.
